assertions.expectation: Identifies PDB as the most likely/common cause of stuck upgrades
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'that's the most common culprit for stuck upgrades' and lists PDBs as the first and primary issue to check

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl command to check PDBs (kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces or similar)
assertions.reason: Provides multiple PDB commands including 'kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces' and 'kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces -o wide'

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify which pods are blocking the drain
assertions.reason: Includes commands to check pods that can't be evicted, pods stuck in terminating state, and specific kubectl commands to identify problematic pods

assertions.expectation: Includes resource constraint diagnosis (capacity issues, pending pods)
assertions.reason: Section 5 includes 'kubectl describe nodes' and 'kubectl top nodes' commands to check for resource constraints and capacity issues

assertions.expectation: Provides a fix for restrictive PDBs (patch or temporary adjustment)
assertions.reason: Provides specific kubectl patch command to adjust PDBs and suggests temporary deletion as an option

assertions.expectation: Includes guidance on surge upgrade settings as a potential fix
assertions.reason: Section 6 includes gcloud command to configure max-surge and max-unavailable settings for future upgrades

assertions.expectation: Mentions webhook issues as a possible cause
assertions.reason: Response does not mention webhook issues at all as a potential cause of stuck upgrades

assertions.expectation: Includes validation steps to confirm the fix is working
assertions.reason: While it mentions the upgrade should resume automatically within 5-10 minutes, it doesn't provide specific commands or steps to validate that the fix is actually working
